content: Just as curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (ICM/RND) shape what an autonomous agent chooses to explore, 2025 cutting-edge active learning agent memory systems can leverage a "curiosity gatekeeping" principle to prioritize storing only high-uncertainty, information-dense experiences that drive future learning, rather than archiving all observed data. This replaces the standard uniform memory retention approach with a curiosity-aligned pruning strategy that both reduces memory bloat and improves downstream task performance.
